page: Microsoft Azure F16alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance provides 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with a 1890 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-threaded performance, with gzip compression reaching 41.2 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 17.76 GB/sec, both placing in the top tier. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is also strong at 249.41 GB/sec. Memory performance is robust, with a Passmark memory score in the top 25% and a small-block cached read bandwidth of 3215.91 GB/sec. Large-block read and write bandwidths measure 116.72 GB/sec and 73.34 GB/sec, respectively. In LLM inference tests using a small 135M model, the server achieves a top-tier text generation speed of 401.18 tokens/sec and a strong prompt processing speed of 3384.44 tokens/sec.

Given its compute-heavy profile with a lower memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require high clock speeds and raw CPU throughput rather than massive memory footprints. The inclusion of a large 1890 GB local disk offsets the need for external storage attachments for data-intensive operations. This server is suited for compute-intensive tasks, single-threaded compression workloads, lightweight LLM inference, and applications requiring high-frequency processing with moderate memory requirements.
description: Microsoft Azure F16alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 32.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core) and a 1890 GB local disk with Premium Storage support. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-threaded performance, with gzip compression at 41.2 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 17.76 GB/sec. It also demonstrates strong memory performance and efficient small-model LLM inference, reaching 401.18 tokens/sec for text generation. Lacking a GPU, this instance is qualitatively cost-effective for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory allocations. It is highly suited for compute-intensive tasks, data compression, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F16alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs at 4.5 GHz, 32 GB of RAM, and a 1890 GB local disk, delivering top-tier single-threaded compression performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F16alds_v7 is a compute-optimized instance with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 1890 GB local disk for high-throughput CPU workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency AMD EPYC compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated cores, fast single-threaded compression, and substantial local storage.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 cores running at 4.5 GHz
32.0 GB system memory with a 2.0 GB per core ratio
1890 GB local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-threaded compression and decompression performance
Strong small-model LLM inference text generation speeds
